title: porpax jerdoniana
intro: Porpax jerdoniana, also known as the Indian pipe orchid or Jerdon's Pipe Orchid, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to the Western Ghats of India. It is a type of epiphyte that grows in the wild, often in shaded areas and near water sources. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to help with wound healing, fever reduction, and immune system enhancement. Herbal preparations of Porpax jerdoniana include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
